name: two conflicting birthdays hold BOTH sides and file attribute_collision
description: Adversarial attribute-collision / split-detection probe. Two notes assert a different birthDate (attribute kind) for the SAME entity Cleo. Per supersession.decide attribute branch, neither side wins: BOTH active heads move to pending_review and an attribute_collision review fires (a 'two birthdays is a bug, not news' guard). Pins that an attribute conflict never auto-supersedes — both candidate values are held, exactly one attribute_collision is raised, and no value is silently active. DOCUMENTED GAP (not xfail, since the implemented surfacing is attribute_collision): docs/reference/ANALYSIS.md 'Split detection' says conflicting attributes should propose an ENTITY SPLIT (hidden two-people merge), but the pipeline has no entity_split review kind — it only files attribute_collision and never proposes re-resolving the mention spans into two entities. The deterministic hold-both behaviour is asserted here; the missing split proposal is the residual risk.
